channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Miranda v. Arizona: The Supreme Court case that made the Miranda Rights
item.description: In 1966, the Supreme Court ruled that police must inform suspects of their Miranda Rights before speaking about the incident, following the case of Miranda V. Arizona in 1963, in which a suspect was denied legal representation and his constitutional rights were violated.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Los Angeles wildfires will transform the 2025 Grammys
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Grammy Awards will look a little bit different this week. Each year, the Recording Academy hosts a multitude of events to welcome the music industry during Grammy week and record labels do the same.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump fills his government with billionaires after running on a working-class message
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s brash populism has always involved incongruence: the billionaire businessman-politician stirring the passions of millions who, regardless of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New top prosecutor for DC advocated for Jan. 6 rioters and echoed Trump's false 2020 election claims
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For years, conservative activist Ed Martin has promoted Donald Trump's false claims about a stolen 2020 election, railed against the prosecution of the rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol and represented some of them in court.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kash Patel’s podcast persona: staunch Trump defender and fierce critic of the FBI he could soon lead
item.description: Kash Patel positioned himself as a steadfast Donald Trump loyalist well before the president picked him to run the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Ali Swenson And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In ‘2000 Meters to Andriivka,’ Oscar winner takes viewers back to Ukraine’s frontlines
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — The day Mstyslav Chernov won the BAFTA for his documentary “20 Days in Mariupol” was the day he learned two soldiers he knew had been killed in combat.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KREX 5

item.title: DOJ fires prosecutors who worked on Trump criminal cases
item.description: Justice Department officials fired several prosecutors who worked on President Trump's criminal cases, saying they could not “trust” them.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: Rain douses wildfires in Southern California without causing serious mudslides
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The first significant storm of the season brought snow and downpours to Southern California that doused wildfires and caused some ash and mud to flow across streets in the Los Angeles area on Monday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Woman gets more than 3-year prison term for helping pregnant Chinese women get to US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California woman was sentenced Monday to more than 3 years in prison in a long-running case over a business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who automatically became American citizens. U.S. District Judge R.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Full-scale replica of Anne Frank's hidden annex opens in NYC
item.description: A full-scale replica of the secret annex where Anne Frank penned her famous diary has opened in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo And Joseph Frederick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American mom held in UK for allegedly killing two kids can return to US for harsher sentence: report
item.description: Kimberlee Singler's youngest children, 7 and 9, were found dead in their Colorado home in December 2023. A United Kingdom judge ruled that she can return to the United States for trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter pardoned by Trump killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KTLA 5

item.title: Prosecutors say woman charged in killing of Border Patrol agent was in contact with homicide suspect
item.description: A Washington state woman charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. border patrol agent in Vermont had been in frequent contact with someone whom authorities have linked to homicides in Pennsylvania and California, a federal prosecutor said in court documents Monday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3.8 magnitude earthquake in Maine felt in Massachusetts, Rhode Island
item.description: A Ware, Massachusetts, resident said she was home and her whole house was shaking for about 10 seconds from what she at first believed was an 18-wheeler coming down Route 9.
item.dc:creator: Emma McCorkindale, KRON4

item.title: Sinaloa cartel member arrested by ICE in Texas: sources
item.description: A Sinaloa cartel member arrested by ICE in El Paso, Texas, was found with multiple weapons and thousands of rounds of ammunition, sources say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Person seen firing gun into air amid crowd of Philadelphia Eagles fans; police investigating
item.description: Video of the alleged incident, shared to X late on Sunday, appeared to show a large crowd celebrating the Eagles' win when a person raised the gun into the air.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Houston-area school districts address immigration enforcement policies amid federal crackdowns
item.description: In response to President Trump's heightened immigration policies, Houston-area schools and law enforcement are clarifying their protocols regarding federal agency requests, prioritizing student safety.
item.dc:creator: Rilwan Balogun, Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: North Carolina troopers under investigation, about 180 traffic cases dismissed
item.description: District Attorney Lorrin Freeman said she "concluded that there were statements that were made by that trooper that appear to be false and misleading.
item.dc:creator: Mary Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Starbucks offering free refills to all customers, bringing back condiment bar
item.description: Starbucks is implementing new and old policies at its cafes in the United States and Canada to improve the customer experience. Beginning on Monday, customers who order a “for here” beverage can also get free refills of hot or iced coffee or hot or iced tea during their visit.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Is cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Graham: Senate needs to investigate Trump decision to pull Bolton’s security
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) says the Senate needs to investigate President Trump’s decision to end his former national security adviser John Bolton’s personal security protection.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Owner of NYC Israeli restaurant targeted with antisemitic graffiti has message for the vandals
item.description: The owner of an Israeli restaurant in New York City that was targeted by antisemitic graffiti thinks the vandals got the "opposite" of what they hoped for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters take to the Texas Capitol after ICE operations in Austin
item.description: On Sunday afternoon, a crowd of protestors gathered at the Texas Capitol after U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement raids were conducted in Austin.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Torres, NewsNation

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors say woman charged in killing of Border Patrol agent was in contact with homicide suspect
item.description: Federal prosecutors say a Washington state woman charged in connection with the fatal shooting of U.S. border patrol agent in Vermont had been in frequent contact with someone whom authorities have linked to homicides in Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Acting deputy AG head visits Chicago to 'observe' immigration enforcement
item.description: Chicago residents, especially in immigrant circles, have already been on edge for months in anticipation of large-scale immigration arrests promised by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oklahoma bill would make it a crime punishable by jail time to "possess" a shopping cart belonging to a business
item.description: An Oklahoma lawmaker is proposing a law that would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in jail for anyone caught “in possession of” a shopping cart taken from a store.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, KRON4

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5
